analysis.consistency: High. The spell details (level, school, components, casting time, range, duration, and class list) are standard for a 2nd-level D&D 5e spell like Lesser Restoration.
analysis.quality: High. The description is concise and directly states the mechanical effect. The use of Markdown links/anchors within the description (e.g., `[blinded](#blinded)`) indicates an attempt to link to external condition definitions, which is good for digital presentation but structurally part of the content delivery, not the core mechanics.

mechanics.effect: Ends one of the following conditions on a touched creature: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

analysis.recommendations: No mechanical changes necessary. The data accurately reflects the spell's function. If this data is for a system that does not support in-line linking, the anchors (`#condition`) should be removed for pure mechanical parsing, but they are acceptable for display.
